affected_groups: children whose support depends on funding formulas
content.decision_question: Should Hungary redesign its SEN finance architecture based on plausible incentive theory even though the causal link to Hungary's identification rise remains unestablished, or wait for stronger domestic evidence before altering funding rules that schools currently depend on?
content.evidence_boundary: Further evidence, such as natural experiments around rule changes or cross-district comparison, could test whether Hungary's specific multipliers causally drive identification. It cannot resolve whether the value of fiscal caution should outweigh the value of proactively removing a plausible distortion before proof is complete.
content.tension: Shifting from diagnosis-gated to census-based finance (TP-live-t4) rests on the plausible theory that fiscal incentives drive over-identification, but no causal evidence links Hungary's specific financing mechanism to its identification rise, and Poland's contrasting experience of expanding rather than removing disability-weighted funding while separately managing gatekeeping shows the theorized link is not universal.
content.title: Finance-incentive reform: theory-driven change versus unproven causal mechanism
value_poles: caution against acting on unproven mechanisms
